description: TEV protease recognition site, engineered ENLYFQG heptapeptide (P1'=Gly) — cleaved between Q and G by Tobacco Etch Virus NIa protease; canonical scar-minimal tag-removal site.

references.authors: Dougherty WG, Carrington JC, Cary SM, Parks TD
references.title: Biochemical and mutational analysis of a plant virus polyprotein cleavage site
A viral cleavage site cassette: identification of amino acid sequences required for tobacco etch virus polyprotein processing

references.authors: Parks TD, Leuther KK, Howard ED, Johnston SA, Dougherty WG
references.title: Release of proteins and peptides from fusion proteins using a recombinant plant virus proteinase

references.authors: Kapust RB, Tozser J, Fox JD, Anderson DE, Cherry S, Copeland TD, Waugh DS
references.title: Tobacco etch virus protease: mechanism of autolysis and rational design of stable mutants with wild-type catalytic proficiency

references.authors: Kapust RB, Tozser J, Copeland TD, Waugh DS
references.title: The P1' specificity of tobacco etch virus protease

references.authors: Phan J, Zdanov A, Evdokimov AG, Tropea JE, Peters HK 3rd, Kapust RB, Li M, Wlodawer A, Waugh DS
references.title: Structural basis for the substrate specificity of tobacco etch virus protease

label: TEV_site
note: Tobacco Etch Virus (TEV) NIa protease recognition site, engineered form ENLYFQG (Glu-Asn-Leu-Tyr-Phe-Gln-Gly). TEV protease cleaves the scissile peptide bond between the P1 Gln and the P1' residue, i.e. after the Q (ENLYFQ|G), leaving a single non-native Gly on the released downstream product. Whole-part feature spanning all 7 residues; this is a single atomic functional element with no functional sub-features. P1'=Gly is an engineered variant of the natural NIb|CP junction sequence ENLYFQS (P1'=Ser).

provenance.sequence_source: Engineered P1'-Gly variant of the Tobacco Etch Virus NIa protease cleavage site. ENLYFQG is the de-facto standard recombinant tag-removal TEV site (single non-native Gly scar). Defining literature: Parks et al. 1994 (Anal Biochem, PMID 8179197; founding use of the TEV site for fusion-tag removal) and Kapust et al. 2002 (Biochem Biophys Res Commun, PMID 12074568; P1' specificity characterizing the Gly form). The natural NIb|CP junction is ENLYFQS (UniProt P04517 aa 2786-2792); ENLYFQG differs only at P1' (S->G), captured in the sequence_variant claim.

functional_claims.label: ENLYFQ(G/S) is the 7-residue TEV protease recognition site; the enzyme cleaves between the P1 Gln and P1' (after the Q, ENLYFQ|G)

value.protease: Tobacco Etch Virus NIa protease (TEV protease, EC 3.4.22.44)
value.cleavage_position: between P1 Gln and P1' residue (ENLYFQ|G)

source.quote: Biochemical and mutational analysis of a plant virus polyprotein cleavage site

functional_claims.label: The stored sequence is the engineered P1'-Gly variant (ENLYFQG); the natural TEV NIb|CP junction is ENLYFQS (P1'=Ser)

value.natural_origin: TEV genome polyprotein, NIb|CP cleavage junction (UniProt P04517 aa 2786-2792 / GenBank M15239 frame+1)
value.rationale: P1'-Gly is a well-established engineered form: it is accepted by TEV protease and leaves a single non-native Gly on the cleaved product; both ENLYFQG and ENLYFQS are widely used recombinant TEV sites

source.quote: The P1' specificity of tobacco etch virus protease

functional_claims.label: TEV protease tolerates several residues at P1' (Gly, Ser, Ala, Met, Cys, His) but not Pro; this defines the difference between the ENLYFQG and ENLYFQS variants

value.consequence: the P1' residue becomes the new N-terminal residue of the released downstream product; ENLYFQG leaves an N-terminal Gly, ENLYFQS leaves an N-terminal Ser

source.quote: The P1' specificity of tobacco etch virus protease

functional_claims.label: Engineered into a fusion-protein linker, ENLYFQG enables highly specific proteolytic release of the passenger from its tag by TEV protease

value.founding_demonstration: use of the TEV (ENLYFQ-S/G) site to release proteins/peptides from fusion proteins with a recombinant plant-virus proteinase
value.advantage: high sequence specificity (7-residue recognition) gives clean, minimal-scar tag removal

source.quote: Release of proteins and peptides from fusion proteins using a recombinant plant virus proteinase

functional_claims.label: The crystal structure of TEV protease with substrate/product peptide explains the heptapeptide selectivity and confirms cleavage between Q and the P1' residue

value.structure: TEV protease complexed with a substrate/product peptide
value.explains: structural basis of ENLYFQ(G/S) heptapeptide selectivity
value.confirms: scissile bond is between P1 Gln and P1' residue

source.quote: Structural basis for the substrate specificity of tobacco etch virus protease

functional_claims.label: Host-agnostic sequence element; the cleaving enzyme (TEV protease) is supplied separately, so the site functions independently of the expression host

value.host_dependence: none (cleavage performed with TEV protease, in vitro or co-expressed in vivo)
value.enzyme_origin: Tobacco Etch Virus NIa protease (commonly the S219V-stabilized recombinant form)

source.quote: Tobacco etch virus protease: mechanism of autolysis and rational design of stable mutants with wild-type catalytic proficiency
